reasoning: Although the patient demonstrated positive TTG-IgA serology (36.54 units on 2020-06-12), a definitive celiac disease diagnosis requires confirmatory histopathological evidence per the clinical decision rules. A systematic review of all 239 clinical notes identified no matching phrases for increased intraepithelial lymphocytes, villous blunting or atrophy, a Marsh 3/4 grade, or external biopsy confirmation. The available pathology documentation explicitly stated that "the examined duodenum was normal," confirming intact villous architecture and unremarkable lymphocyte counts. Because no positive histological signals were detected, the "no pathological signal found" rule was triggered across all notes, satisfying the Negative outcome criteria due to the complete absence of required biopsy evidence.
